sub.question: What is the Vicharanashala internship?
sub.answer: A two-month internship run by Vicharanashala, a research lab at IIT Ropar. You will work on a real open-source project under a mentor, after a short training phase tailored to where you already are. The internship is free — we do not charge, and the work is real.

sub.question: What is VINS?
sub.answer: VINS is the Vicharanashala Internship — an online programme open to anyone who clears our interview. The work is real open-source contribution under a mentor, the certificate is from the Vicharanashala Lab for Education Design at IIT Ropar, and the programme itself is free (we charge nothing). There is no stipend. If you are seeing a yellow VINS panel on your result page, you are selected.

sub.question: What are the phases of VINS, and what do the badges mean?
sub.answer:
VINS is structured as four phases. Each one is marked by a badge:

🥉 Bronze (Phase 1) — a short training period at the start, planned around what you already know. If you arrive already comfortable with the basics, your mentor may skip Bronze and put you straight on to the project.

🥈 Silver (Phase 2) — the main work. You contribute to a real open-source project under a Vicharanashala mentor. Finishing Bronze and Silver completes your internship and earns the certificate.

🥇 Gold (Phase 3) — a recognition awarded during Silver if your contribution stands on its own as a meaningful feature, not just a small fix.

🏆 Platinum (Phase 4) — a standing invitation to come back and visit the lab — a short trip — any time during the year after your internship ends. We help with travel through a small visit stipend.

Most interns finish at Bronze + Silver, and that is exactly what the certificate is for. Gold and Platinum are extras you can pick up if your work makes the case for them.

sub.question: Who is the internship for? Are alumni eligible?
sub.answer:
The internship is for currently-enrolled students at any college or university — undergraduate, postgraduate, or doctoral. The NOC requirement is the practical reflection of this: we ask for institutional consent that you can commit your time to this internship.

Candidates who have already graduated and are not currently enrolled in any programme are not eligible for this cycle. If you re-enrol later (higher studies, etc.), you are very welcome to apply again in a future cycle.

sub.question: Is this the same as IIT Ropar's official Summer Research Internship?
sub.answer: No. Summership 2026 is a VLED Lab initiative. The certificate is issued by the Vicharanashala Lab for Education Design, not centrally by the institute. IIT Ropar runs a separate institutional summer research internship through its own office. Do not represent Summership 2026 as equivalent to that programme.

sub.question: I have to attend my class tomorrow/today/some day — can I take leave?
sub.answer: Leave is not permitted. If you are also attending classes or exams, you will be relieved from the internship immediately and will need to join the next batch when it starts.

sub.question: When can I start?
sub.answer:
You can start any time in 2026 — VINS is flexible on the start date — but there are two things you must hold in mind together, and one strong recommendation.

The hard rule: Your internship must finish by 31 December 2026. That date is non-negotiable. Whatever start you pick, your end date (your start + 2 months, with up to 1 month grace) must land on or before 31 December 2026. So while there is no last date to opt in, there is absolutely a last date to finish.

The strong recommendation: start as soon as possible. The earlier you join, the more of the May–July main cohort you catch — cohort networking, TA support concentrated in May–July, and training that rolls out with the cohort rather than as a static document all make starting earlier materially better.

sub.question: How long is the internship?
sub.answer: Two months from your chosen start date, with an optional one-month grace period if you need it. End must land on or before 31 December 2026.

sub.question: Can I start in July, August or later if I have exams now?
sub.answer: Yes — but only if your exams genuinely make an earlier start impossible. Wait until your exams are done, then opt in and start. Do not attempt to juggle this internship with ongoing exams. Make sure your chosen start date plus 2 months (or 3 with grace) lands on or before 31 December 2026.

sub.question: Can I start with the cohort and take a relaxation during my exam window?
sub.answer:
No. This is not an arrangement we offer. VINS is a full-attention internship — six to ten hours a day, sometimes more. Splitting that with college exams damages both sides. If your exams fall inside the cohort duration, defer your start to after your exams end, opt in then, and run the internship at full attention.

Note: If we later learn that a candidate was sitting college exams during their internship period, we reserve the right to terminate the internship or withhold the certificate at any time — including after the internship has otherwise been completed.

sub.question: Can I take leave or get an exemption during the internship for an exam scheduled in June?
sub.answer: The attendance rule is firm — the 55-day continuous window is a non-negotiable part of the internship, and we cannot offer an exemption for an exam during this period. The policy exists because split attention genuinely damages both your exam preparation and your internship work.

sub.question: Are orientation session recordings shared with interns, and can project or group assignments be changed after watching them?
sub.answer: Recordings of the sessions will not be provided. However, we may provide access to an abridged version of a talk or session if we consider it important. We do not guarantee this for every session.

sub.question: What dates do I put on the NOC?
sub.answer: Default: your chosen start date → your start + 2 months (with up to 1 month grace), ensuring the end date is on or before 31 December 2026. Pick the earliest start date you can realistically make — the May–July summer window is the main cohort. If the NOC will be signed on a specific later date, pick a start date after the signature date.

sub.question: Who can sign the NOC?
sub.answer: Any authorised signatory at your college: HOD, Acting HOD (during holidays), Principal, Dean, Director, or Training & Placement Officer. For dual-degree students, either institution can sign — pick whichever is easier. For IITM BS Online Degree (standalone) students, any officer from the BS office can sign.

sub.question: When do I submit the NOC? Is the deadline hard?
sub.answer: The deadline is not hard — there is no specific cut-off date by which the NOC must be uploaded. But for the internship to actually begin properly, you should submit it as early as possible and join the current summer cohort. If you are on VINS you can technically upload your NOC and start later in the year, but we strongly do not recommend it — by then your mentor may already be busy, and the cohort + TA support that make this internship genuinely good are concentrated during the summer window.

sub.question: What format should I use? Do I need to design it myself?
sub.answer: No — we provide a printable NOC format. Once your result is out and you log in to samagama.in, you will see a 'Download blank NOC' button on your dashboard. Take a printout, get it physically signed and stamped by your authorised signatory, scan it, and upload the signed PDF using the 'Upload signed NOC' button. You do not need to draft anything yourself, and you do not need college letterhead.

sub.question: What if my college / Program Chair gives me an NOC in their own format?
sub.answer: A college's own NOC format is acceptable, as long as all four of the required entries are present: (1) the signing authority's handwritten signature, (2) the signing authority's official email address, (3) your full name, and (4) your signature. If your college's format does not include a place for your signature, sign clearly and prominently anywhere on the document before uploading.

sub.question: Does it need to be signed by hand?
sub.answer: Yes. Three things are required: the authorised signatory's handwritten signature, the institutional rubber stamp / seal applied in the designated area, and the signatory's email address filled in the designated field — we automatically cross-check with that person to verify the signature is genuine. Digital signatures are not accepted on the PDF path. If a physically-signed printout is impractical for your HOD, use the email-forward path (see §3.7) — it is fully equivalent.

sub.question: Can my HOD email the NOC instead of signing a printout?
sub.answer:
Yes — there is a fully-equivalent email-forward path. How it works: (1) From your dashboard, download the text NOC. (2) Fill in the student-side fields, then email the file to your HOD and ask them to forward it. (3) Your HOD forwards the email to sudarshan@iitrpr.ac.in from their official institutional email address, with the subject line: 'NOC for my student <Your Full Name>'.

Two non-negotiable conditions: the forward must come from the HOD's official institutional email address (not a personal Gmail or Outlook account), and the subject line must start with 'NOC for my student'. After the forward arrives, confirmation and offer-letter issuance follow the normal timeline (about 24 to 48 hours).

sub.question: How do I download and upload the NOC?
sub.answer: Both happen on your dashboard at samagama.in once your result is out. You will see a NOC section with two buttons available in three places: (1) a compact pill in the dark header bar, (2) a standalone NOC card on the dashboard, and (3) a NOC section at the bottom of your full Result message. The two buttons are 'Download blank NOC' (saves the printable NOC format PDF) and 'Upload signed NOC (PDF)' (opens a file picker; the file must be a PDF of at most 1 MB).

sub.question: What if my NOC is not formally verified?
sub.answer: NOC verification takes time — typically anywhere between an hour and one full working day from the moment you upload. If you are planning to start in the coming days and need your offer letter sooner, there is a tentative route: upload a self-declaration on your profile and a tentative offer letter will be issued to you immediately. The formal offer letter follows once your NOC clears verification.

sub.question: My online course (Masai, NPTEL, Coursera, etc.) won't issue an NOC. What do I do?
sub.answer:
The internship is open only to candidates currently enrolled in a full-time degree programme at a recognised college or university. Online-only courses — Masai Institute, NPTEL / MOOC enrolments, Coursera, Udacity, bootcamps, and similar — do not by themselves make a candidate eligible.

If you are concurrently enrolled in a full-time degree programme alongside the online course, please obtain a No Objection certificate from that college and upload it. If your only current academic engagement is the online course and you are not concurrently enrolled in a full-time degree programme, the internship is not open to you in this cycle.

sub.question: My HOD/college official wants written confirmation before signing my NOC. What do I show them?
sub.answer: Your selection is already confirmed the moment your yellow VINS result panel appears on your samagama.in dashboard. If your HOD insists on a document in hand before signing, use the tentative offer letter route: (1) Log in to samagama.in and open your profile. (2) Upload a brief self-declaration. (3) A tentative offer letter on Vicharanashala letterhead is issued to your dashboard immediately. (4) Hand the tentative offer letter to your HOD — it serves as the written confirmation they need to sign your NOC.

sub.question: Can Prof. Sudarshan Iyengar or a faculty member from IIT Ropar sign my NOC for the internship?
sub.answer: No. Your NOC must be signed by an authorised signatory at the institution where you are enrolled as a student — such as your HOD, Dean, Principal, or Training & Placement Officer. Sudarshan Iyengar is a faculty member at IIT Ropar and is not the authorised signatory for the IIT Ropar/Masai online AIML programme. He cannot sign your NOC in a personal capacity. If your only current academic enrolment is the IIT Ropar/Masai online programme, you are not eligible for this internship cycle.

sub.question: How do I know I am selected?
sub.answer: If you can see your yellow VINS result panel on samagama.in, you are selected. There is no separate selection step or confirmation email.

sub.question: How do I opt into VINS?
sub.answer: Tell Yaksha in the chat: 'I want to take up the online internship without stipend.' Yaksha will confirm. Opting in is the selection — no separate confirmation email is sent at that stage.

sub.question: When do I get the offer letter?
sub.answer:
There are two paths:

Path 1 — Formal offer letter (default): Issued once your signed NOC has been verified AND you have confirmed your internship start and end dates on the dashboard. The formal offer letter is issued automatically — usually within an hour of the second step completing.

Path 2 — Tentative offer letter (faster): If you cannot wait for NOC verification, upload a self-declaration on your profile instead. A tentative offer letter will be issued immediately, and the formal offer letter follows once your NOC clears verification.

Either letter is on Vicharanashala letterhead and serves as your official document for college / employer records.

sub.question: Where do I find my offer letter?
sub.answer: The offer letter lives on your dashboard at samagama.in, not in your email. When it is issued you will receive a notification email from noreply@vicharanashala.ai with the subject 'Your Vicharanashala Offer Letter is ready — please log in to download.' That email is only a notification; the actual letter is downloaded from the Offer Letter card on your dashboard. If you do not see the notification email, check your spam / promotions folder.

sub.question: Will I get a certificate?
sub.answer: Yes — every intern who completes the internship gets a certificate from Vicharanashala, IIT Ropar. The internship is genuinely demanding; candidates who drop out mid-way do not get a certificate. Finishing means something, because the bar is high.

sub.question: How do I confirm my internship dates?
sub.answer: Once you have opted into VINS in the chat with Yaksha, log in to samagama.in. On the dashboard, you will see a yellow card titled '🗓 Confirm your internship dates'. The two date pickers pre-fill with sensible defaults for the current cohort. If those work for you, hit 'Save dates'. Otherwise edit them to your earliest realistic start — your end must be on or before 31 December 2026. A green confirmation appears once saved. You can edit any time from the same card. Order doesn't matter — you can save your dates before or after uploading your NOC.

sub.question: I am a minor/major in AI student, can I join the programme? I don't need a NOC as I am from IIT Ropar.
sub.answer: Minor/Major in AI course from IIT Ropar is a certification course and there will be a different track of internship equivalent to them. Kindly write to us separately for this. For you to be part of this internship programme you should be a registered student in a UG/PG programme with some university. This internship is exclusively meant for students only and not for working professionals.

sub.question: How do I accept the offer letter?
sub.answer:
Reply to the offer-letter email you received from no-reply@vicharanashala.ai. In the body, paste the following acceptance statement exactly as printed, with your full name inserted and a date added:

'I, [Full Name], confirm that I have read, understood, and accepted all terms, conditions, and obligations set out in this offer letter and in the program FAQ at samagama.in. I formally accept the offer of Summer Internship 2026.'

Do not paraphrase, do not shorten, do not rearrange the words. The reply must reach us within 5 days of the offer letter being sent.

Alternatively, you may download the offer letter PDF, fill in your name and the date in the acceptance block, sign and scan as a PDF, and attach that signed file to your reply.

sub.question: What if I reply without using the exact acceptance format printed in the letter?
sub.answer: The offer is withdrawn, effective immediately, with no further correspondence. The withdrawal is final. This is a deliberate policy — the acceptance statement is the first attention-to-detail check of the internship. What counts as non-compliant: paraphrasing the statement, sending only a bare 'I accept', missing the date, missing the FAQ-reference clause, or an attached photo of an unfilled offer letter. Minor single-word slips or obvious typing mistakes in an otherwise complete attestation are forgiven.

sub.question: I received a withdrawal email because I didn't accept the offer letter correctly. Can it be reversed?
sub.answer: There is an appeal path, with conditions. Do not reply to the withdrawal email itself — replies are not read. To appeal, send a fresh email to sudarshansudarshan@gmail.com with the subject line exactly: 'Request to Reconsider: Confirmation Reply Error'. In the body, state an apology for the mistake and the reason. If the reason is genuine, we will respond within 24 hours. An appeal that is granted places you on a separate track with an additional assignment: a short course on attention to detail, which you must complete and clear before the internship can proceed.

sub.question: What happens after I send my acceptance? My dashboard doesn't update.
sub.answer: The dashboard tracks your NOC, your internship dates, and your offer letter — it does not track the acceptance email. After you send your acceptance reply, you will not see a new green tick or status change on the dashboard. This is normal and expected. We process acceptance emails manually. If your reply was compliant, no further action is needed. If several working days pass and you have heard nothing, log in to samagama.in and type #escalate in the Yaksha chat.

sub.question: How do I change my internship dates before the offer letter is issued?
sub.answer: Until your formal offer letter has been generated, you can change your start and end dates yourself from the dashboard at any time — open the 'Confirm Internship Dates' card under My Journey and edit the dates. Your end date must still be on or before 31 December 2026. This self-edit path is available only until the offer letter is generated. Once the offer letter has been sent, the dates are locked and the dashboard edit no longer works.

sub.question: When and how do I get the Zoom link for the kickoff meeting?
sub.answer: The kickoff orientation is held for the main summer cohort only — candidates starting at the opening of the May–July window. The Zoom link is delivered via email to your registered samagama.in address, and also through your Yaksha chat portal. If your start date is later (mid-summer or beyond), there is no separate kickoff event for you. If you cannot register with the Zoom link or have not received it, log in to samagama.in and type #escalate in the chat.

sub.question: How to proceed if NOC is not available and my chosen internship start date is approaching? I did not receive a selection confirmation letter too.
sub.answer: If your start date is approaching, do not wait for the selection confirmation letter. Use the self-declaration form to obtain your provisional offer letter. You have a 15-day window to submit the proper NOC and get an actual offer letter. Failure to submit the NOC within this window may affect your internship.

sub.question: When does my internship actually begin? Will I receive a notification on the day?
sub.answer: Your internship begins on the start date you confirmed on the dashboard — the same date printed on your offer letter. There is no separate 'your internship has begun' notification on the day itself. On the morning of your start date, log in to samagama.in. Yaksha will guide you through the Day-1 steps of the Bronze phase. If your dashboard appears unchanged on that morning, do a hard refresh and re-login. If it still looks the same, type #escalate in the chat.

sub.question: Can I switch from VINS (online) to VISE (offline) after being selected?
sub.answer: No. The two tracks are finalised at the interview stage, and we do not move candidates between them. VISE has a fixed on-campus capacity planned around mentor bandwidth, hostel availability, and stipend allocation — once the shortlist is set, it stays set. VINS is not a consolation track. The project, the mentor, and the certificate are the same as VISE — what differs is the mode (online) and the absence of a fellowship.

sub.question: Can I change my internship dates after the offer letter has been issued?
sub.answer: Yes — but not through the dashboard self-edit path, which is locked once the offer letter is issued. To request a change, the official from your institution who signed your NOC must send an email to harshdeep.r@vicharanashala.ai (CC: sudarshan@iitrpr.ac.in) with the subject 'Date change request — Your Full Name — Vicharanashala Summership 2026', including the reason and new proposed start and end dates. Once received, the Confirm Internship Dates card will be unlocked and a re-issued offer letter will be generated. Requests sent directly by the student cannot be processed at this stage.

sub.question: What will I work on?
sub.answer: A real open-source project from Vicharanashala's portfolio — assigned based on your background and the lab's current needs. Areas range across AI/ML, web development, NLP, computer vision, agriculture-tech (Annam.AI), education-tech (ViBe), and open-source infrastructure. We do not pre-publish the exact problem; you choose to join knowing the lab will assign the project.

sub.question: How many hours per day?
sub.answer: Plan for 6 to 10 hours a day, sometimes more during the build phase. This is a full-time internship for the two-month window. Most candidates who drop out are juggling something else — VINS expects your full attention.

sub.question: Who is my mentor?
sub.answer: You will work with the lab's research and engineering team. The exact mentor depends on the project. The model is fluid — you will get help from a senior researcher one day, a peer the next, and someone else for a different question. That is how real open-source work happens.

sub.question: Is there a stipend?
sub.answer: No. The internship is unpaid. Stellar performers may be recognised with a discretionary stipend at the lab's option, but this is not promised or expected.

sub.question: Do I need my own laptop? Should I preload any software?
sub.answer: Yes — a personal laptop is required. We prefer that you bring a laptop running Linux or macOS. If you use Windows, please install a terminal that can SSH and run Unix-style commands — for example, Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) is a clean choice; a tool such as PuTTY also works. We do not maintain a fixed software-preload list — your mentor will guide you on the specific tools needed once your project is assigned.

sub.question: I am using a different email on GitHub / Zoom / the learning platform. Is that okay?
sub.answer: No. Your registered email is your sole identifier across all programme platforms. Progress tracking, mentor assignment, and certificate issuance are all tied to it. Mismatches between platforms cannot be retroactively corrected — ensure you use your registered email everywhere from day one.

sub.question: Why has my mentor not been assigned yet, or contacted me on day 1?
sub.answer: Mentors are not assigned on day 1 of the internship. You will be assigned a mentor when you move on to the project phase of VINS, which comes later in the timeline. Before that, you must complete the mandatory coursework of the Bronze phase. Once coursework is complete and you are placed on a project, your mentor will reach out. Note: we do not run a Discord server. Communication is through Yaksha chat on samagama.in and email from noreply@vicharanashala.ai only.

sub.question: Are unofficial WhatsApp groups, Telegram channels, or peer-run groups allowed?
sub.answer:
No — unofficial channels of any kind are strictly not allowed. All communication with Vicharanashala about this internship must flow through official channels only: (1) Yaksha chat on samagama.in (start a message with #escalate to reach a human), (2) email notifications from no-reply@vicharanashala.ai, and (3) the moderated WhatsApp troubleshooting group, which we invite you to after your offer letter is issued.

Creating, joining, or operating an unofficial group — a parallel WhatsApp group, a Telegram channel, a Discord server, or any other peer-coordinated space — is strictly prohibited. Candidates found to be running or actively participating in such unofficial channels may face withdrawal of the offer letter, removal from the internship, blacklisting from future Vicharanashala programmes, and a formal communication to your college or placement office.

sub.question: My interview is not marked as complete on the dashboard — what do I do?
sub.answer: A data-sync issue sometimes occurs where the chat session closes but the interview record doesn't update to 'completed.' The team will check your record and manually mark it as complete if needed. You will be unblocked within 1–2 hours. If you don't hear from us and your interview continues to be marked incomplete, please write to us at sudarshansudarshan@gmail.com.

sub.question: Does Vicharanashala send a grade report or evaluation to my university for internship credit?
sub.answer: Vicharanashala does not send formal evaluation or grade reports to universities — that process is between you and your college. The certificate issued upon completion is the document you can submit to your college or placement office for credit. Whether your college accepts it and how they translate it into a grade is their decision. We can provide the certificate and, if earned, a letter of recommendation, but we don't have a process for sending grade reports to universities.

sub.question: Does the Vicharanashala internship certificate specify whether it was completed online or offline?
sub.answer: The certificate you receive on completing the internship is the same for both tracks. It is issued by Vicharanashala, IIT Ropar, and does not specify whether you completed it online or on campus. The certificate records only that you completed the internship; the mode is not called out separately on the document.

sub.question: Will the completion certificate be a physical hardcopy or an e-certificate?
sub.answer: The completion certificate is issued as an e-certificate — you download it from your dashboard on samagama.in after completing both Bronze and Silver. We do not print and mail physical copies. The certificate is digitally signed and authentic, so it cannot be duplicated. It can also be verified from our database using the number on the certificate.

sub.question: Is there a WhatsApp group for candidates during the internship?
sub.answer: There is no WhatsApp group for this internship. All communication happens through official channels that have been or will be communicated to you through email notifications. We use WhatsApp for intermittent issue handling and delete them after the task. Not being on WhatsApp should not make you miss anything.

sub.question: What is Rosetta?
sub.answer: Rosetta is your internship journal — a 65-day document, one entry per day, every day, for the full duration of Summership 2026. You write in it daily, keep it privately, and submit it at the end of the internship as one of your completion requirements. The name comes from the Rosetta Stone — discovered in 1799, it carried the same text in three scripts and became the key to decoding an ancient language. Sixty-five days of honest writing will become the key to understanding your own experience.

sub.question: Why does this exist? Is it just busywork?
sub.answer: No. It exists for two reasons. For you: the journal builds articulation of what you learned and what changed in you, one day at a time — students who reflect regularly consistently get more out of a programme than those who do not. For us: when you submit Rosetta at the end, it gives us qualitative insight into your experience that no survey can capture. We use it to understand what worked, what did not, and how to make the programme better for the next cohort.

sub.question: What is a 'thinking routine'?
sub.answer: Each day in Rosetta has a thinking routine — a short framework that gives your reflection a specific shape. Examples include: 3-2-1 (3 things you engaged with, 2 questions on your mind, 1 surprise), Muddy / Clear (what is sharp and what is still foggy), and What? So What? Now What? (separating facts from meaning from action). The routines rotate across the 65 days so the journal does not feel repetitive or mechanical.

sub.question: How do I get my Rosetta journal?
sub.answer: Your journal will be shared with you as a Google Doc template link during orientation. Open the link, make a copy to your own Google Drive, rename it 'Rosetta — [Your Name] — Summership 2026', and that copy is yours for the full 65 days. Do not write in the shared template. Always work in your own copy.

sub.question: How do I use it day to day?
sub.answer: Simple: (1) Open your Rosetta Google Doc. (2) Scroll to the entry for today's day number. (3) Fill in the date at the top of the entry. (4) Read the thinking routine name and its one-line description. (5) Answer the three prompts in the writing boxes below. (6) Close it and get on with your day. It should take between 10 and 20 minutes. It is not an essay. It is not a report. It is a journal.

sub.question: How long should each entry be?
sub.answer: There is no minimum or maximum word count. A good entry is one that is honest and specific. Three to five sentences per prompt is usually enough. If you find yourself writing more because something genuinely needs unpacking, write more. If a day was quiet and you genuinely only have two sentences, that is fine too. What is not acceptable: one-word answers, copy-pasted text, vague non-answers like 'today was productive', or anything that reads like it was generated by an AI.

sub.question: What is the one rule?
sub.answer: Write what is true. Not what sounds impressive, not what you think we want to read, not a polished summary of the day. If you hated today, write that. If you are confused and frustrated, write that. If something clicked and you are genuinely excited, write that. We will know immediately if an entry reads like an LLM wrote it. The journal only counts as a completion requirement if it is genuinely yours — in your voice, from your actual experience.

sub.question: Can I use ChatGPT or any AI tool to write my entries?
sub.answer: No. This is the one firm rule of Rosetta. The journal is a record of your thinking, not a demonstration of what an AI can produce on your behalf. Entries that read as AI-generated will not be counted toward your completion requirement. If your entire journal reads this way, the journal will not be accepted. Use AI tools for your technical work if that is permitted in the programme. Do not use them here.

sub.question: What if I miss a day?
sub.answer: Fill it in as soon as you can. Write the actual date you are filling it in, not the date of the missed entry. Be honest in the entry about the fact that you are writing it late and why. Do not leave entries blank. A late honest entry is always better than no entry. If you find yourself consistently missing entries, it usually means something else is going wrong — use Yaksha in chat, or reach out to your scholar.

sub.question: Will anyone read my journal during the internship?
sub.answer: No. We will not access your journal during the 65 days. You write it, you keep it, it is yours. The only time we read it is after you submit it at the end of the internship. This is intentional — we want you to write freely, without feeling observed. The journal is only useful if it is honest, and it is only honest if you are not performing for an audience.

sub.question: Can the prompts change mid-internship?
sub.answer: Occasionally we may update a prompt for a specific day based on what is happening in the cohort. When this happens, we will announce it through the programme communication channel before that day begins. Check the announcement channel before filling any entry where a change has been announced. If no announcement has been made, use the prompt as written in your document.

sub.question: How do I submit Rosetta at the end?
sub.answer: On or before Day 65, share your Rosetta Google Doc with the programme coordinator's email address (shared during wrap-up week). Set the sharing permission to Viewer. Make sure: your name is in the document title, all 65 entries have been filled in, and your cover page has your name, product, and team filled in. Rosetta submission is one of the required criteria for receiving your internship certificate. An incomplete or AI-generated journal will not be accepted.

sub.question: I have a question about Rosetta that is not answered here. What do I do?
sub.answer: Ask Yaksha first. If Yaksha cannot answer it, escalate to your programme coordinator. Do not sit on a question — the journal works best when you start it right.

sub.question: My college requires a written confirmation that the internship is self-paced and will not clash with college classes — what document can I share with them?
sub.answer: This is not a self-paced internship, but a very rigorous one which is time demanding. It is not permitted for one to be part of any other activity during this period.

faq.main: Phase 1 — coursework, Vibe LMS, and live sessions

sub.question: I've previously interned with VLED — am I exempt from any coursework?
sub.answer: Yes — partially. If you previously interned with VLED and completed the MERN Stack coursework, you don't need to repeat it this cycle. However, the AI Fundamentals course (Fundamentals of AI using an Agriculture dataset) is a new addition this cycle and is mandatory for everyone, including returning interns. To claim the MERN exemption, type the hashtag #exemption from mern stack course in your Yaksha chat on Samagama. Note: the broader #exemption from coursework (viva-route) option is not available to returning interns.

sub.question: How do I register for the AI Fundamentals course on Vibe?
sub.answer: Follow these steps: (1) Click the AI Fundamentals registration link sent in the Phase 1 launch email. (2) You'll be redirected to the Vibe sign-in page. If you don't have a Vibe account yet, create one using the same Gmail you used to register on Samagama. (3) Log in with your credentials. (4) After logging in, open the course registration link again in your browser — the second click after login is what enrols you. (5) Complete the brief registration form and submit. (6) The course will appear instantly on your Vibe dashboard, ready to watch. Please use the same email on both platforms.

sub.question: I registered on Vibe with a different email than my Samagama email — is that OK?
sub.answer: Vibe requires a Gmail address for signup. If the email you used on Samagama is not Gmail (e.g. a college email), you may use any Gmail of yours to register on Vibe. In that case, tell Yaksha in your Samagama chat using the tag: #vibe-email your-gmail@gmail.com so we can link the records.

sub.question: Are live sessions mandatory if I'm on the viva route?
sub.answer: Yes — live sessions are mandatory for every intern, regardless of path. Whether you're on the coursework track, MERN-exempt, or have cleared the viva and moved to Phase 2, you're expected to attend every live session. The exchange of knowledge across our cohort — diverse streams, varying levels of experience — is something self-paced study cannot replicate.

sub.question: Where do I find the daily live-session schedule?
sub.answer: We share each day's schedule through any one of these channels: Samagama dashboard, Yaksha chat, or email. Please keep an eye on all three — on a given day the schedule may go out via any one of them.

sub.question: I'm unable to type in the chat after clicking 'Interact with Yaksha' — what should I do?
sub.answer: After logging into your Yaksha portal, if the field shows that you can't type, scroll up to the top of your window and click on the 'Chat with Yaksha' button to activate Yaksha. This way you will be able to interact with Yaksha properly.

sub.question: How do I log in to ViBe?
sub.answer: Link for the website: https://vibe.vicharanashala.ai/auth. Sign up as a student with the registered mail ID into the ViBe platform. To log in: (1) Log in to the ViBe platform as a student from your registered email ID. (2) Check the 'Notifications' tab in the top right side of the Dashboard. (3) Accept the course invite sent for your respective MERN Course. Warning: Logging in with a different email ID may result in access issues or missing course visibility.

sub.question: Invite accepted but shows 'No course enrolled'?
sub.answer:
Make sure you are logged in with the correct registered email ID. Check if your college email has multiple aliases and try the correct one. Log out and log in again once. Use personal wifi instead of college wifi as there might be some network restrictions of access.

If the issue continues: (Step 1) Allow Third-Party Cookies — enable cookies in Chrome, turn off 'Block third-party cookies', add .vicharanashala.ai to site exceptions, and restart browser. (Step 2) Fix DNS — change your laptop DNS to Google DNS: Preferred DNS 8.8.8.8, Alternate DNS 8.8.4.4. (Step 3) Flush Old DNS Cache — open Command Prompt as Admin and run: ipconfig /flushdns, ipconfig /release, ipconfig /renew, then restart WiFi.

sub.question: Why are videos stuck or repeating?
sub.answer: This may happen due to ViBe's monitored learning system. Common reasons include: videos must be watched fully and in sequence (no skipping), camera and microphone permissions must be enabled, poor lighting or high background noise can affect playback, and switching tabs or staying idle may restart the video. For smooth playback, stay on the ViBe tab, keep your camera on, and watch videos in a quiet, well-lit environment.

sub.question: Can I use a mobile or tablet?
sub.answer: No, only desktop/laptop is supported.

sub.question: I'm experiencing video issues (stuck, looping, skipping) on ViBe. How do I troubleshoot?
sub.answer: Try these troubleshooting steps in order: (1) Refresh the page and check multiple times. (2) Inspect browser console: Right-click → Inspect → Go to Network or Console tab → Try watching the video and check for any visible errors. (3) Log out and log in again. (4) Use a different browser. (5) Clear browsing data and cache, then try to re-login. If the issue persists after trying all steps, record the issue and contact Yaksha by mentioning #escalate-ViBe.

sub.question: I have completed all videos and quizzes in the ViBe course, but my progress is still showing less than 100%. What should I do?
sub.answer: Please do not worry. This might be a skip made in the quiz/video item due to penalty score as the quiz/video item might not have been successfully completed/marked. Please verify that you've completed all the course items (1006/1006). If not, please retry the missed contents again. In the meantime, try: (1) Refresh your browser. (2) Log out, clear your browser cache, and log in again.

sub.question: I feel the ViBe content or platform is not good or I am unhappy with the way progress is evaluated. Can I request an exception or bypass the system?
sub.answer:
ViBe is built and continuously improved by interns and students themselves. It is a free and open-source learning platform.

If a learner strongly feels that the regular ViBe flow does not fairly reflect their understanding, there is a formal alternative evaluation path: watch the specified YouTube video content completely, appear for a three-hour proctored examination with two cameras (front and side view) and one online human proctor, then: score below 60% means you must join the next cohort; score between 60% and 80% gives you one more chance; score above 80% means you have passed. This special exam is conducted once every fortnight, so most students find continuing with the standard ViBe workflow is the faster and better option.

sub.question: Is the ViBe consent form compulsory? What if I don't want to grant camera access?
sub.answer: Yes — the consent form is compulsory. Providing consent is a mandatory requirement for any candidate enrolling in a course on the ViBe Learning Platform. The platform is designed with proctoring enabled throughout the learning process, which requires access to your webcam and microphone. If you choose not to grant camera and microphone access, you will not be able to proceed with the course. Note: ViBe does not continuously record videos — proctoring operates via real-time monitoring mechanisms only.

sub.question: What are penalty scores on the ViBe platform, and how do they affect our performance or HP?
sub.answer: Penalty scores are generated when anomalies are detected during your activity on the ViBe platform (e.g., irregular behavior while watching video lessons or attempting quizzes). If the penalty score becomes high, you may be required to rewatch the video lesson and retake the associated quiz. At present, these penalty scores do not impact your HP (Health Points) or overall performance evaluation, as they are not being considered for scoring. Their primary purpose is to ensure proper engagement with the learning content.

sub.question: When should I use the Flag option on ViBe, and when should I contact support?
sub.answer: Use the Flag feature on ViBe only for course content-related issues, such as problems with video content or quiz questions. For technical issues, platform errors, login problems, or logistics-related queries, do not use the flag option. Instead, contact Yaksha so the team can assist you faster.

sub.question: What is Linear Progression on ViBe?
sub.answer: Linear progression is enabled for every course on ViBe. This means each learner must watch the videos and attempt the quizzes in the exact order they appear on the left panel of the course page. You cannot click on a video or quiz that lies far ahead of your current position. You must complete each item before the next one unlocks. Skipping videos or quizzes is not allowed by design. Progress is sequential — finish the item in front of you, and the next one opens up automatically.

sub.question: Can I use the left navigation panel to jump ahead to a later video or quiz?
sub.answer: No. Although the left navigation panel displays the full list of items in your course, it is meant only as a progress map — not as a jump-to-anywhere menu. Instead of clicking through the left panel, click 'Next Quiz' or 'Next Lesson' as it appears on the right panel. Let the platform unlock items for you in sequence as you complete each one. Trying to skip ahead through the left panel will trigger the Access Restricted alert.

sub.question: I am seeing a red 'Access Restricted' banner. Is this a bug?
sub.answer: No, this is not a bug. The red Access Restricted banner is an intentional alert from the platform. It appears when you try to open an item (video or quiz) before completing all the items that come before it. When the banner appears, ViBe automatically returns you to the previous valid content — that is, the last item you had legitimately reached in the sequence. You will not lose any progress; you'll simply be sent back to where you actually are in the course.

sub.question: How do I resolve the 'Access Restricted' error?
sub.answer: Follow these steps in order: (1) Go back to the left panel and scroll through your course items from the beginning. (2) Look for any item without a completion tick — that is your missed video or quiz. (3) Complete that item (watch the full video, or attempt and submit the quiz). (4) Refresh the page once you've completed all earlier items. (5) If the Access Restricted banner still appears after refreshing and you are sure all earlier items are completed, report the issue to Yaksha by mentioning #escalate-ViBe.

sub.question: Why does ViBe sometimes make me re-watch a clip after a quiz?
sub.answer: If your answer to the check-in quiz didn't go through correctly, ViBe will take you back to the same clip and let you try again. This is called a re-watch, and it is part of the design — not a penalty. Re-watches are not recorded against you — they do not affect your HP or evaluation. The clips are short, so a re-watch usually costs less time than guessing through multiple retries. Think of the re-watch as the platform helping the idea actually stick before you move on.

sub.question: What kinds of quiz questions will I see on ViBe?
sub.answer: ViBe quizzes come in four formats: (1) Pick one (MCQ) — one right answer out of four or so options; read all options before clicking. (2) Pick one or more (MSQ) — 'Select all that apply'; read each option carefully. (3) Type a number (NAT) — type just the number, no units or extra symbols unless the question explicitly asks. (4) True or False — read the statement carefully; a single word like 'always', 'never', or 'only' can flip the correct answer. Tip: watch the clip first, then answer.

sub.question: Are the same proctoring rules applied to every course on ViBe?
sub.answer: No. ViBe's proctoring system is modular. Each individual check — face visibility, single-face-in-frame, lighting, background voices, gaze on screen, camera/microphone access — can be independently switched on or off by the instructor for a given course or cohort. The instructor decides which proctoring elements are active for their course. Please do not assume rules carry across courses. When in doubt, ask your instructor or programme coordinator which proctoring elements are active for your current course.

sub.question: What does the 'quiet helper' on ViBe actually do?
sub.answer: While a lesson plays, a small helper runs gently on your device using your camera and microphone. It checks, in real time, that the basic conditions for learning are present. Specifically, it checks: (1) a face is visible, (2) only one face is in frame, (3) there is enough light on your face, (4) the room isn't full of voices, and (5) you are looking at the screen. The helper is not a judge. Brief, normal movements (a stretch, a sneeze, a glance at your notebook) are absolutely fine. It only pays attention to sustained patterns, not split-second things.

sub.question: Does ViBe record long videos of me while I'm learning?
sub.answer: No. ViBe does not continuously record videos of you. The camera and microphone are used for real-time presence checks only. Long recordings of your face or voice are not stored. When the lesson ends, the helper goes quiet too. All data is handled strictly in accordance with the consent terms shown when you signed up.

sub.question: What is the single most common avoidable mistake learners make?
sub.answer: Sitting with a window directly behind you during the day. The room may feel bright to you, but your camera only sees a dark silhouette where your face should be. The helper then struggles to confirm your presence, and your lesson may pause or rewind. The fix is simple: move so the window is to your side or in front of the laptop, not behind you. A lamp placed in front of you works just as well in the evening.

sub.question: Why does the lesson keep pausing or restarting even when I'm paying attention?
sub.answer: The cause is almost always something small in your environment. Run through this checklist: your face is too dark (add a lamp in front of you), your face is partly out of frame (raise the laptop or sit closer to the camera), there are voices in the background (close the door or move to a quieter room), you switched tabs or went idle (stay on the ViBe tab; take breaks between clips, not during them), or camera/mic permission dropped (check the lock icon in your browser's address bar and confirm both are set to 'Allow').

sub.question: Can I read the quiz questions aloud or mutter to myself while watching?
sub.answer: It's best not to. The microphone listens for sustained voices in the room, and reading aloud, muttering, or asking a roommate questions can all be picked up as anomalies. The simple habit is: watch in silence, answer in silence, and chat freely during your breaks between clips.

sub.question: Can I study with a friend on camera since we're learning together?
sub.answer: In most courses, no. Only you — the registered Learner — should be in the camera frame during a lesson. The helper checks that exactly one face is visible at a time. A good way to do group study: hop on a separate call with your friend on your phone or a second device; discuss concepts before or after a ViBe lesson, not during it; return to ViBe alone when you're ready to watch the clip and attempt the quiz. Note: the single-face-in-frame check is an instructor-controlled setting — a few courses may explicitly relax this rule, but do not assume that relaxation in one course applies to another.

sub.question: Will I lose my progress if I clear my browser or reinstall it?
sub.answer: No. Your progress is saved on the server, tied to your registered email — not on your browser or your computer. Refreshing the page, clearing cache, switching browsers, or even reinstalling your browser will not wipe your progress. The moment you log back in with the same registered email, all your completed clips and quizzes will be exactly where you left them. When in doubt: log out, log back in.

sub.question: Is there a recommended daily learning rhythm on ViBe?
sub.answer: Yes — small, regular sessions work far better than long marathon sessions. Show up daily, even if only for thirty minutes. Take breaks between clips, not during them. Treat each quiz as a gentle check-in, not a high-stakes test. For programmes with deadlines (such as internships or faculty cohorts), aim for the daily progress target announced by your programme team — typically around 3.33% per day. Always follow the milestone schedule communicated for your specific cohort.

sub.question: What should my 'study corner' look like before I start a ViBe session?
sub.answer: A ViBe-friendly study spot needs only three things: (1) Light in front of your face — a lamp or window facing you, never behind. (2) Just you in the camera frame — ask family, friends, or pets not to wander through. (3) A reasonably quiet room — no TV, no music with words, no one else on a call nearby. Two minutes of setup before you start saves a lot of small frustrations during the lesson.

sub.question: Is team formation compulsory?
sub.answer: Yes. All projects in Phase 2 and Phase 3 (some parts) must be completed in teams. Every participant is required to be part of a team.

sub.question: What is the size of a team?
sub.answer: The team size is fixed at four members. This is mandatory — you cannot have fewer or more members in a team at the time of final formation.

sub.question: How are teams formed?
sub.answer: For students who joined on May 15 and 16: Teams were formed through a structured activity. For students joining later: Teams will be randomly assigned by the administration.

sub.question: I started on May 15/16 but couldn't form a team during the activity. What happens now?
sub.answer: You will be randomly assigned to a team. No action is required from your side. The administration will verify and match email IDs with names before finalizing and locking teams.

sub.question: There was a typo in our email addresses during team formation. Can we fix it?
sub.answer: No. Teams with fewer than four members will be expanded by adding additional members to make a final team of four. The administration will attempt to assign a replacement member. If no replacement is found, you may continue as a team of three. You must inform the admin immediately, or the change will not be recognized.

sub.question: I formed a team with only two members. Will it be considered?
sub.answer: No. Teams with fewer than four members will be expanded by adding additional members to make a final team of four.

sub.question: What if a team member leaves or becomes ineligible during Phase 1?
sub.answer: The administration will attempt to assign a replacement member. If no replacement is found, you may continue as a team of three. You must inform the admin immediately, or the change will not be recognized.

sub.question: Can I form a team with someone from my own college?
sub.answer: No. Teams must consist of members from different institutions to encourage networking. Exception: Students from the same institution but different campuses/locations may be allowed.

sub.question: Can I form a team with students from my IIT MBS cohort?
sub.answer: No. You are encouraged to collaborate with participants outside your existing cohort.

sub.question: Can we change our team name after submission?
sub.answer: Yes, team names are tentative and can be changed. However, due to operational constraints, frequent changes are discouraged. Teams will be distinguished using suffixes (e.g., Team X-1, Team X-2, etc.).

sub.question: What if multiple teams choose the same name?
sub.answer: Teams will be distinguished using suffixes (e.g., Team X-1, Team X-2, etc.).

sub.question: What should I do if I face issues within my team?
sub.answer: Report any concerns immediately to your assigned scholar/mentor. Maintaining a safe and respectful environment is a priority.

sub.question: How will I know who my mentor is?
sub.answer: Your mentor will be the scholar assigned to the project your team is working on.

sub.question: When will I know my team details?
sub.answer: Teams are announced in phases via email. Please regularly check your inbox for updates. Team announcements are phased, so your name may appear in a later list. If your entire cohort has moved to team activities and you are still unassigned, raise the issue on Yaksha or contact a scholar.

sub.question: I received a team list email but my name is not included. What should I do?
sub.answer: Team announcements are phased, so your name may appear in a later list. If your entire cohort has moved to team activities and you are still unassigned, raise the issue on Yaksha or contact a scholar.

sub.question: We selected Project X as our top priority but were assigned Project Y. Can we change it?
sub.answer: No. Project assignments are final and cannot be changed. Allocation is done to ensure balanced distribution across projects.

sub.question: I just started the internship. Can I form my own team now?
sub.answer: No. For later cohorts, teams will be randomly assigned. Please wait for the official communication.

sub.question: When do team activities begin?
sub.answer: Team-based work begins in Phase 2. During Phase 1 (online coursework), you do not need to worry about team activities.

sub.question: Can I request a specific teammate after teams are assigned?
sub.answer: No. Team assignments are final and requests for changes are not entertained.

sub.question: What happens if a team member is inactive or not contributing?
sub.answer: You should report the issue to your mentor/scholar early. Prolonged inactivity may lead to administrative intervention. Team switches are not allowed except in exceptional, admin-approved cases involving serious concerns.

sub.question: Can I switch teams if there are conflicts?
sub.answer: Team switches are not allowed except in exceptional, admin-approved cases involving serious concerns.

sub.question: Will team performance affect individual evaluation?
sub.answer: Yes. While some components may be individual, team deliverables are a key part of evaluation.

sub.question: How will communication happen within teams?
sub.answer: Teams are expected to self-organize internal team coordination (e.g., a small WhatsApp/email thread limited to the four teammates). Official programme updates will continue to come via email and Yaksha chat on samagama.in. Note: this in-team coordination is distinct from the unofficial cohort-wide channels prohibited under the code of conduct — a four-person team thread for project work is fine; any group that purports to act on behalf of Vicharanashala or the wider cohort is not.

sub.question: What if I miss the team allocation email?
sub.answer: All communication is sent via registered email. You are responsible for checking regularly, including spam folders.

sub.question: Can a team be dissolved and reformed?
sub.answer: No. Once finalized, teams are locked and cannot be dissolved.

sub.question: What happens if I drop out of the internship?
sub.answer: Your team will be adjusted accordingly, and the remaining members may continue as a team of three or receive a replacement.

sub.question: Will we get time to get to know our teammates before Phase 2?
sub.answer: Yes. There is typically a buffer period before Phase 2 where teams can connect and prepare.
